tldr: Two ads are running identical body copy and two more are duplicate creatives on different PDPs — that's 4 of 5 ads carrying fatigue risk inside 14 days. Expect CPMs to drift 18-25% higher over the next 3 weeks unless the rotation gets refreshed.

benchmark.context: OHTNYC's average creative lifespan is half the streetwear/jewelry category median. The duplicate body copy across Ads #2/#3 and identical creative on #4/#5 is accelerating audience burnout.

ads.headline: For Your Weekly Jewelry Rotation
ads.body: Be unapologetically you. ✧˖°⋆｡˚  Every piece is meticulously designed to empower you to express your unique style fearlessly.
drivers: Body copy is 100% duplicate of Ad #2 — Meta's algorithm is splitting frequency across twins
Generic empowerment line ('unapologetically you') has zero product or NYC-specific hook
Same LP as Ad #2 (transparent heart charm) compounds creative cannibalization

ads.headline: Layered to perfection
ads.body: Be unapologetically you. ✧˖°⋆｡˚  Every piece is meticulously designed to empower you to express your unique style fearlessly.
drivers: Body copy duplicates Ad #3 verbatim — auction overlap is real
'Layered to perfection' headline doesn't match a transparent heart charm LP — message-match break
No price, no urgency, no proof — relies entirely on aesthetic

ads.headline: From CUBE to Cosmos — The OHT x LIGHTSUM Drop
ads.body: ₊˚⊹♡ OHT x CUBE LIGHTSUM ♡⊹˚₊  From CUBE to cosmos — a collection inspired by bold legacies and bright energy. Built for the main character era. Limited drop, made to shine on and off stage. ✧˖°⋆｡˚
drivers: Identical creative to Ad #4, only LP differs (supernova vs cube dice necklace)
K-pop collab angle has a hard ceiling — LIGHTSUM fan audience saturates fast
'Limited drop' urgency loses teeth the longer it runs without a countdown

ads.headline: From CUBE to Cosmos — The OHT x LIGHTSUM Drop
ads.body: ₊˚⊹♡ OHT x CUBE LIGHTSUM ♡⊹˚₊  From CUBE to cosmos — a collection inspired by bold legacies and bright energy. Built for the main character era. Limited drop, made to shine on and off stage. ✧˖°⋆｡˚
drivers: Twin of Ad #5 — frequency is doubling on the same fan-base segment
Collab-specific copy has a built-in expiration once the drop ages out
No social proof, UGC, or stage footage to extend the narrative

ads.headline: Soft Enough for Daily. Sharp Enough to Feel Like You
ads.body: ₊˚⊹♡ THE USUAL ♡⊹˚₊  Not the jewelry you save for 'someday.' The ones you live in, grocery runs, late nights, everything between. Soft, sharp, and oht to the core ♡  Meet the usual, your everyday, upgraded ☁︎⚙︎☆
drivers: Strongest concept in rotation — specific use-case ('grocery runs, late nights')
Headline contrast hook ('Soft Enough... Sharp Enough') is genuinely thumb-stopping
Only ad with a clear positioning ('the usual') vs collab/aesthetic copy

ad_to_scale.headline: Soft Enough for Daily. Sharp Enough to Feel Like You
ad_to_scale.body: Not the jewelry you save for 'someday.' The ones you live in, grocery runs, late nights, everything between.
ad_to_scale.why: This is the only ad doing real positioning work — 'the usual' as a category claim is ownable, and 'grocery runs, late nights' makes the buyer picture wearing it tomorrow. Lowest fatigue score, longest runway, and the easiest to extend into a 4-5 ad concept family. Push budget here while the other four cool off.

ad_to_kill.headline: For Your Weekly Jewelry Rotation
ad_to_kill.body: Be unapologetically you. ✧˖°⋆｡˚ Every piece is meticulously designed to empower you to express your unique style fearlessly.
ad_to_kill.why: Body copy is identical to Ad #2 and points at the same PDP — you're paying Meta twice to compete with yourself. The 'unapologetically you' line is interchangeable with any DTC jewelry brand and gives the algorithm nothing to optimize toward. Kill it this week and reallocate to Ad #1.

concepts.hook: Same earrings. Seven outfits. Zero takeoffs.
concepts.primary_text: ₊˚⊹♡ THE USUAL, tested ♡⊹˚₊ One pair of Shooting Stars. Seven days straight — shower, gym, subway, dinner, sleep. No tarnish, no green ears, no 'someday.' This is the jewelry you actually live in. Soft enough for daily. Sharp enough to feel like you. ☁︎⚙︎☆ Shop The Usual.
concepts.visual_direction: Day-counter overlay (Day 1 → Day 7), real girl in NYC apartment, bodega, train, rooftop. Close-ups on earrings staying put. Phone-shot, not studio.
concepts.fills_gap: Zero UGC or durability proof in current rotation — everything is aesthetic flat-lay energy.

concepts.hook: Why I started OHT 6 blocks from Canal Street.
concepts.primary_text: ₊˚⊹♡ Built downtown, not in a deck ♡⊹˚₊ OHT started because the jewelry I wanted didn't exist — pieces with NYC attitude that survive a real week. Every cut-and-sew, every charm, every chain gets sampled six blocks from Canal. This is the usual, made by us, worn by you. Meet the brand behind the rotation.
concepts.visual_direction: Founder talking head walking through SoHo/Canal, b-roll of sample room, fabric, jewelry trays, sketches. Handheld, golden hour.
concepts.fills_gap: No founder, brand-story, or NYC-specific creative — current ads could be from any aesthetic DTC brand.

concepts.hook: Build your stack in under 60 seconds.
concepts.primary_text: ₊˚⊹♡ The Stack Builder ♡⊹˚₊ Frame 1: Start with The Usual. Frame 2: Add the Heart Charm. Frame 3: Layer the Cube. Frame 4-5: Mix, match, repeat. Frame 6: Your rotation, locked. Every piece is meticulously designed to layer — no clashing, no green skin, no rules. Build yours ♡
concepts.visual_direction: Flat-lay → on-model progression. Each frame adds one piece. Clean white + one OHT pink accent. Price chips on each frame.
concepts.fills_gap: No carousel format and no AOV/bundle play in rotation — every current ad is single-product.

unlocks: Cut wasted spend on the duplicate Ad #2/#3 pair — projected 15-20% efficiency lift on the heart charm SKU
Extend Ad #1's 'The Usual' positioning into a 3-ad concept family before it hits day 24
Add UGC and founder formats to break the all-flat-lay pattern Meta is currently learning
Stop the LIGHTSUM collab from dragging account-level frequency once the drop window closes

next_step.urgency: Ad #3 hits fatigue in ~4 days, Ad #2 in ~6. The window to swap is this week.
next_step.headline: Let's lock the next 3 creatives before Ad #3 dies
next_step.body: Book 30 minutes and we'll walk through the scale plan for 'The Usual,' the kill order on the duplicates, and which of the 3 concepts to ship first. You'll leave with a dated rotation calendar.

method_note: Scores blend copy-duplication signal, LP overlap, format monotony, and category fatigue benchmarks across public Meta Ad Library data — directional, not impression-weighted.
